title: English Metrical Homilies
summary: CME00029 is the repository copy of a University of Michigan electronic selection from John Small's 1862 English Metrical Homilies. The encoding retains the edition title page and the verse text on printed pp. 1-171: a prologue, the Ratio, and the first thirteen Northern Homily Cycle homilies from Advent through the Purification. Small's principal source was the Northern Homily Cycle portion of the Royal College of Physicians of Edinburgh manuscript known as the MS of Cursor Mundi, with explicitly delimited supplies and readings from Cambridge University Library MS Gg.5.31 and Oxford, Bodleian Library MS Ashmole 42. A complete print scan and derivatives, plus Anne B. Thompson's complete 2008 scholarly edition, are cached. No complete public digital object was found for any of the three physical witnesses; Small's three-snippet printed plate is only a partial facsimile of the Edinburgh codex.
review_notes: The edited selection, complete 1862 publication, U-M encoding, repository XML, three physical manuscripts, complete print scan, and partial manuscript plate are distinct entities.

Small's introduction and notes control the witness dependencies. His principal Edinburgh manuscript has no verified current shelfmark beyond the scholarly designation MS of Cursor Mundi; no number is invented.

The cached print PDF is complete relative to the 1862 volume. CME00029 itself maps discontiguously to the title page and printed pp. 1-171, omitting the intervening plate and preliminaries and the notes on pp. 173-188.
Whole-object manuscript access checks include physical-object surfaces and adjacent works. A selected public plate or text-bearing folio range is not treated as a complete codex surrogate.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00029
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow.
notes: The file is 174,812 bytes and is pinned through the recorded CME submodule commit.
TEMPHEAD records a 2016-05-26 structural and proofing review, not the first creation date of the U-M encoding.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item CME00029
entities.description: A digital edition-level selection containing the 1862 title page and printed pp. 1-171. It omits the facsimile plate, Small's introduction and manuscript description, a blank printed p. 172, the notes on pp. 173-188, and other scan matter.

date_statements.label: repository XML review
date_statements.notes: A review date for this serialization, not a claimed first-keying or digital-publication date.

entities.label: John Small, English Metrical Homilies (1862)
alternate_labels: English Metrical Homilies from Manuscripts of the Fourteenth Century
entities.description: Small's edition of the opening fragment of the Northern Homily Cycle, with an introduction, a three-snippet manuscript plate, the verse text through the Purification, and textual notes. The continuous copy text is principally Edinburgh, Royal College of Physicians, MS of Cursor Mundi, but substantial losses are supplied from Gg.5.31 and Ashmole 42.

bibliographic.citation: John Small, ed., English Metrical Homilies from Manuscripts of the Fourteenth Century, with an Introduction and Notes (Edinburgh: William Paterson, 1862).
bibliographic.title: English Metrical Homilies from Manuscripts of the Fourteenth Century, with an Introduction and Notes

notes: CME00029 encodes the title-page content and printed pp. 1-171 only.

The volume calls itself printed from the Edinburgh manuscript, but its notes identify several continuous supplied runs from the Cambridge and Ashmole witnesses; the edition is therefore not modeled as a homogeneous transcription of one codex.

entities.label: Edinburgh, Royal College of Physicians, MS of Cursor Mundi
alternate_labels: Royal College of Physicians of Edinburgh manuscript of Cursor Mundi
entities.description: An early-fourteenth-century vellum miscellany of fifty leaves in three textual fragments and hands. Its Northern Homily Cycle portion, ff. 16r-36v, preserves the prologue, Ratio, and first thirteen homilies and breaks near the beginning of the second Purification exemplum. Small used it as his principal witness.

holding.notes: No reliable current numbered shelfmark or item-level online catalog record was verified. The Northern Homily Cycle is at ff. 16r-36v; Small's printed plate also samples fols. 1 and 37 from adjacent Cursor Mundi fragments.

physical_description.extent: 50 leaves; the Northern Homily Cycle occupies ff. 16r-36v, with three leaves missing after fol. 24
physical_description.format: Thin quarto miscellany; text in two columns, normally 43 lines, with three fragments or hands; leaves later mounted in cardboard frames and bound
notes: The Northern Homily Cycle section is in one textura hand with Latin Gospel rubrics in red.
The manuscript was bequeathed to the College in 1741 by Dr John Drummond.
The North Riding localization applies to the Northern Homily Cycle copying hand, not automatically to the work's composition or the manuscript's later Edinburgh location.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Gg.5.31
entities.description: An early-fifteenth-century northern vellum witness of the Northern Homily Cycle. Small used it for substantial continuous supplies where the Edinburgh witness is defective and for comparison readings elsewhere.

holding.notes: The manuscript's surviving pages are pencil-numbered according to an original pagination despite losses. Thompson follows those manuscript numbers; earlier descriptions may count surviving pages instead, so a folio/page crosswalk should not be inferred without direct inspection.

physical_description.format: Northern English Northern Homily Cycle manuscript with losses and competing page-count conventions
notes: Small cites this source as Gg V.31 or the Cambridge manuscript.

entities.label: Oxford, Bodleian Library MS Ashmole 42 (S.C. 6923)
entities.description: A mid-fourteenth-century northern vellum manuscript preserving a nearly complete Northern Homily Cycle. Small supplied one isolated reading and a continuous passage from it and collated it for variants.

holding.notes: A current Digital Bodleian object was not found.

physical_description.format: Northern Homily Cycle manuscript in one hand and one column, normally 38 lines
notes: The cycle is nearly complete despite the noted losses.

entities.label: Fac-simile of MS plate in Small's 1862 edition
entities.description: One monochrome printed plate containing three selected manuscript snippets labeled fol. 1, col. 1; fol. 16, col. 1; and fol. 37, col. 1. Only the middle snippet samples the Northern Homily Cycle portion. It is not a whole-manuscript surrogate.
entities.reproduction_format: Monochrome printed plate reproduced within the 1862 volume

entities.label: Harvard/Google/Internet Archive scan of Small's 1862 English Metrical Homilies
entities.description: A complete 229-page Google-digitized scan of a Harvard Library copy, exposed by Internet Archive with PDF, OCR, word-coordinate XML, and page-data derivatives. It contains the entire xxii + 188-page publication, the printed manuscript plate, covers and scan matter.
entities.reproduction_format: Image-only PDF with OCR and page-data derivatives
notes: Complete means the provider's full scanned print object, not preservation-master resolution and not permission for redistribution.
The OCR is useful for navigation but is not relied on for exact medieval readings or shelfmarks.

entities.label: Anne B. Thompson, The Northern Homily Cycle (2008)
entities.description: A modern METS/TEAMS edition and introduction used here to identify the Northern Homily Cycle, describe its manuscripts, distinguish work composition from witness production, and qualify authorship and dating hypotheses. It is later supporting scholarship, not an upstream source of CME00029.

bibliographic.citation: Anne B. Thompson, ed., The Northern Homily Cycle (Kalamazoo: Medieval Institute Publications, 2008).
bibliographic.title: The Northern Homily Cycle

agents.name: John Small
notes: Editor, selector, transcriber, and collator of the 1862 edition; not the medieval author.

assertion.notes: Identifiers, title, structure, and content agree; the current U-M source XML was not independently downloaded for byte comparison.

local_copies.label: Anne B. Thompson, The Northern Homily Cycle (2008), complete downloadable edition

work_portion.label: Modern discussion and edited text corresponding to CME00029
locators: The Present Edition, manuscript descriptions, editorial policy, and manuscript list at PDF pages 13-16 / printed introduction pp. 12-15
Prologue, Ratio, and Homilies 1-13 through the end of Purification at PDF pages 18-84 / printed pp. 17-83
notes: The textual range is an exact intellectual overlap, not a page-for-page facsimile: Thompson supplies a newly edited and normalized text whose witness choices differ from Small's at identified passages.
The complete Thompson PDF continues with selected later-cycle homilies, explanatory notes, appendices, glossary, and bibliography beyond the CME00029 overlap.

rights.component: Underlying 1862 edition text, typography, apparatus, and printed plate
rights.basis: The publication appeared in 1862, and the U-M item applies No Copyright-United States to the encoded public-domain edition materials.
notes: This does not waive separate contractual or provider terms governing modern scan files.

rights.component: University of Michigan digital text and item materials
rights.basis: The item identifies the materials as public domain and displays the No Copyright-United States rights statement for educational and research use.

rights.component: Google-digitized Harvard scan files, OCR, and page data
rights.basis: The underlying 1862 book is public domain, but the scan's embedded Google notice requests noncommercial personal use, attribution, and preservation of legal notices; no open scan-file license was verified.
notes: The ignored source cache is a private research copy and is not a corpus redistribution grant.

rights.basis: The plate was published within the 1862 edition; access to its modern scan remains subject to the separate scan-file terms.

rights.component: Prospective RCPE-supplied or researcher-taken complete-object manuscript photography
rights.basis: RCPE permits personal photography and supplies images for noncommercial personal study or unpublished academic work; publication and commercial reuse require express written permission. No item-specific license exists for images not yet obtained.

rights.component: Prospective Cambridge University Library-supplied complete-object photography
rights.basis: CUL offers consultation, remote viewing, and image enquiries, but no item-specific license for a not-yet-supplied Gg.5.31 digital object was found.
Bodleian offers paid imaging and publishes reuse terms, but no item-specific license applies until images are supplied and terms recorded.

rights.component: Modern scholarly introduction, edition, and downloadable PDF
rights.basis: The modern 2008 scholarly edition is publicly readable and downloadable, but no open-content license was verified on the edition landing page or PDF.
notes: The cached file is supporting research evidence, not content cleared for corpus redistribution.

editorial_practices.source_feature: A Northern Homily Cycle extending through the liturgical year, preserved fragmentarily in the principal Edinburgh manuscript
editorial_practices.representation: Prologue, Ratio, and the first thirteen homilies, from Advent through the Purification
editorial_practices.description: Small published an edited fragment of the Northern Homily Cycle rather than the complete cycle or the complete Edinburgh miscellany.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Losses and defects in the Edinburgh copy alongside corresponding text in Gg.5.31 and Ashmole 42
editorial_practices.representation: A principally Edinburgh text with explicitly noted continuous supplies, isolated supplied words, and comparison readings from the two supplementary witnesses
editorial_practices.description: The edition's immediate source is plural at identified spans. Broad claims that every printed line transcribes the Edinburgh witness are therefore rejected.
editorial_practices.scope: Printed pp. 1-188, especially p. 63 line 17-p. 83 line 18, p. 88 line 8, and p. 167 line 22-p. 171

editorial_practices.source_feature: Complete xxii + 188-page edition with plate, introduction, manuscript description, verse text, and notes
editorial_practices.representation: Title-page content and printed verse text on pp. 1-171 only
editorial_practices.description: The encoding omits the very preliminaries and notes needed to identify the principal witness, supplementary supplies, plate scope, and Small's editorial rationale.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Small's bracketed supplied or editorial text and print typography
editorial_practices.representation: Literal square brackets in line text, with no GAP or UNCLEAR elements and only isolated SUP markup
editorial_practices.description: The XML retains twenty-seven balanced square-bracketed spans as characters rather than structured gap or uncertainty semantics. Its 2016 proofing review does not independently verify those readings against manuscripts.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00029.xml, at CME submodule commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: Primary repository evidence for IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID CME00029; the 2016 TEMPHEAD review; title page; Prologue, Ratio, and thirteen homily divisions; printed-page markers; embedded Latin; bracket characters; and exact repository-file provenance. The file is 174,812 bytes and Git blob 5905076752943f36afa5566a84479ce6fe69b143.

evidence.citation: XPath, text, hash, and division inspection of CME00029.xml.
evidence.summary: Reproducibly finds 16 DIV1 including front and nested Latin divisions, 2 DIV2, 14 HEAD, 17 LG, 4,116 L, 172 PB, 12 NOTE, 4 HI, 1 ABBR, 1 SUP, 0 GAP, and 0 UNCLEAR elements. It also finds twenty-seven opening and twenty-seven closing square brackets in text. The body divides into a 128-line Prologue, 40-line Ratio, and thirteen homilies totaling 3,948 lines.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item CME00029, English Metrical Homilies.
evidence.summary: Official item evidence for the CME/UMDL identity, 1862 Edinburgh imprint, public-domain and No Copyright-United States statements, subjects, and public digital text. The page's bibliographic Author label names John Small; the edition title page and scholarship establish that his role is editor, not medieval author.

evidence.citation: John Small, ed., English Metrical Homilies (Edinburgh: William Paterson, 1862), Harvard Library copy digitized by Google, Internet Archive item englishmetrical00smalgoog.
evidence.summary: Complete 229-page scan directly inspected for title page, imprint, xxii + 188-page extent, plate, preliminaries, printed-text boundaries, blank p. 172, notes, and embedded provider notice. The exact cached PDF is 3,566,547 bytes; complete OCR, coordinate XML, and scan-data derivatives are cached separately.

evidence.citation: Visual inspection and page mapping of the cached Harvard/Google/Internet Archive scan of Small 1862.
evidence.summary: Confirms the title page at PDF page 12 / viewer n11, printed p. 1 at PDF page 36 / viewer n35, and printed p. 171 at PDF page 206 / viewer n205. This establishes the encoding's discontiguous mapping and verifies the complete-volume cache independently of OCR.

evidence.citation: John Small, Introduction, Description of Manuscript, and Notes, in English Metrical Homilies (1862), pp. v-xxii and 173-188.
evidence.summary: Direct editorial evidence that the printed fragment principally follows the Royal College of Physicians manuscript; that Cambridge Gg V.31 supplies p. 63 line 17-p. 77 line 14, p. 88 line 8, and p. 167 line 22-end; that Ashmole 42 supplies words at p. 75 line 9 and p. 77-p. 83 line 18; and that both supplementary manuscripts inform comparison readings. It also describes the Edinburgh codex and preserves Small's superseded authorship discussion.

evidence.citation: Visual inspection of the Fac-simile of MS plate in Small 1862.
evidence.summary: Confirms one printed monochrome plate with three selected snippets labeled fol. 1, col. 1; fol. 16, col. 1; and fol. 37, col. 1. Only fol. 16 belongs to the Northern Homily Cycle portion, so the plate is partial rather than a complete manuscript facsimile.

evidence.citation: Anne B. Thompson, ed., The Northern Homily Cycle (Kalamazoo: Medieval Institute Publications, 2008), introduction and manuscript descriptions.
evidence.summary: Current specialist synthesis identifying the unexpanded Northern Homily Cycle, its liturgical and tripartite homily structure, anonymous and uncertain authorship, proposed c. 1295-1306 composition window, northern or perhaps Yorkshire origin, and manuscript tradition. It describes the Edinburgh fragment at ff. 16r-36v, Gg.5.31, and Ashmole 42 and explains Small's edition as the opening fragment rather than a complete cycle. The cached complete PDF is 1,714,608 bytes.

evidence.citation: Linguistic Atlas of Early Middle English, Chapter 3, manuscript profile discussion for the Edinburgh Royal College of Physicians Cursor Mundi manuscript.
evidence.summary: Specialist linguistic evidence distinguishing three hands and identifying Hand B, ff. 16r-36v, as the Northern Homily Cycle text localized to the North Riding. The localization is retained as a witness-hand dialect claim, not silently promoted to a certain composition place.

evidence.citation: Royal College of Physicians of Edinburgh, Library and Archive, Services and Visiting Us, Research FAQs, and Catalogues and Digitised Material.
evidence.summary: Holding-repository evidence for advance reading-room access, library contact, personal photography conditions, and the scope of listed catalog and digitization resources; it does not promise a complete online surrogate or expose a verified current numbered shelfmark for the manuscript.

evidence.citation: Royal College of Physicians of Edinburgh, Terms of Use, image and reproduction provisions.
evidence.summary: Provider evidence that supplied images are for noncommercial personal study or unpublished academic work and that commercial or published reuse requires express written permission.

evidence.citation: Dated complete-digital-object access check for Edinburgh, Royal College of Physicians, MS of Cursor Mundi.
evidence.summary: The repository's listed digitized collections and catalog resources expose no complete object under the scholarly designation, and exact-designation checks of current public indexes found only textual descriptions and Small's partial plate. This is a dated negative, not proof that no institutional photography exists.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library, Special Collections catalogues, Use Special Collections, Virtual Reading Room, and Manuscripts Services.
evidence.summary: Official evidence that many medieval manuscript descriptions remain in printed catalogs and that onsite consultation, remote curator-assisted viewing, and imaging enquiries are available. It does not establish a public complete Gg.5.31 object.

evidence.citation: Dated complete-digital-object access check for Cambridge University Library MS Gg.5.31.
evidence.summary: Exact-shelfmark checks of CUDL, CUL catalog guidance, Internet Archive, Google Books, and current web indexes did not expose a complete public manuscript object. A guessed CUDL identifier did not resolve and is not treated as a catalog record.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian exact search for Ashmole 42 and Digital Bodleian help documentation.
evidence.summary: The exact Digital Bodleian API-backed search returned totalItems 0 on 2026-07-12. The medieval catalog was not reliably accessible to automated checking. No public complete Ashmole 42 digital object was therefore verified; this is a dated negative.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Order Images, and Digital Bodleian Terms of Use.
evidence.summary: Official evidence for paid imaging requests and provider reuse terms without implying that a complete Ashmole 42 surrogate already exists or has an open license.

open_questions.question: When and by whom was CME00029 first keyed, and is the checked-in XML byte-identical to U-M's current source XML?
next_steps: Retrieve the exact current U-M XML bitstream and compare bytes, structure, identifiers, and revision history.
Keep the 2016 TEMPHEAD date scoped to repository review until production documentation is found.

open_questions.question: What is the current repository shelfmark, and can RCPE supply a complete digital object including covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every manuscript leaf, mounted frames, and adjacent Cursor Mundi fragments?
next_steps: Ask RCPE Library and Archive to identify the manuscript by Small's title, the MS of Cursor Mundi designation, Drummond's 1741 bequest, and the Northern Homily Cycle at ff. 16r-36v.
Request a complete-object inventory and quotation, then map ff. 16r-36v and the three Small plate snippets separately.
Record supplied-file reuse terms before caching or publication.

open_questions.question: Can CUL supply a complete current digital Gg.5.31 object, and how do the manuscript's original pencil page numbers, surviving-page counts, and Small's supplied print ranges crosswalk?
next_steps: Request complete-object photography through CUL, including covers, spine, endleaves, blanks, all pages, losses, and adjacent texts.
Preserve the image labels and both page-count systems before mapping Small p. 63 line 17-p. 77 line 14, p. 88 line 8, and p. 167 line 22-end.
Do not invent folio locators from the printed-page ranges.

open_questions.question: Can the Bodleian supply a complete current Ashmole 42 object, and what exact manuscript folios correspond to Small's p. 75 line 9 and p. 77-p. 83 line 18 supplies?
next_steps: Request a complete-object quotation including covers, spine, endleaves, blanks, all 257 leaves, missing-leaf structure, and adjacent works.
Map Small's isolated and continuous supplies only after direct image or manuscript comparison.
Record item-specific image and publication terms on receipt.

open_questions.question: Which of Small's bracketed supplies, substitutions, and comparison readings can be verified directly against complete current images of all three witnesses?
next_steps: Obtain complete physical-object surrogates through the recorded repository routes.
Collate the printed brackets and note statements against exact manuscript images.
Retain the present intervention description at the level explicitly documented by Small until that work is complete.
